strengths: Strong SQL and Power BI hands-on experience
Quantified impact on KPIs and efficiency (40%, 25%, 300%)
Built end-to-end ETL and data pipelines
Experience with dashboarding and data storytelling
weaknesses: Limited explicit leadership tenure (team lead/manager roles)
Sparse mention of Snowflake/BigQuery production use
Unclear formal mentoring of analysts in high-growth orgs
No explicit experience with revenue assurance/financial reporting
improvement_bullets: Architected robust data modeling strategies in PostgreSQL, defining complex schemas to support scalable KPI development and enterprise-wide reporting requirements.
Engineered scalable data pipelines on cloud platforms (Snowflake/BigQuery), ensuring high data accuracy and reliability for critical business intelligence initiatives.
Mentored junior team members on analytical best practices, fostering a high-performance culture that prioritized accurate KPI development and consistent reporting delivery.
Partnered with leadership to build comprehensive financial performance reports, providing actionable insights that directly supported revenue assurance and operational growth.
Leveraged Snowflake and BigQuery to warehouse large-scale transactional data, translating raw inputs into structured datasets for advanced business performance analysis.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Intelligence Manager opportunity with Atlas Search. I am interested in this opportunity because of my deep passion for scaling analytics functions within high-growth, technology-driven organizations. What interests me about working for Atlas Search is its focus on operational efficiency and data-driven innovation within the infrastructure sector; this commitment to building a sustainable future using rigorous analytics truly inspires me. I believe my hands-on technical expertise in SQL and Power BI, combined with my proven success in cross-functional leadership, would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on the intersection of data integrity and business strategy. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in our customer acquisition process and architected an automated dashboard and reporting solution. By transitioning the team from manual reporting to an automated framework, I reduced processing time by 40% and enabled the executive team to make real-time decisions, directly resulting in a 300% increase in new customer acquisition over 12 months. Ultimately, I know that the Business Intelligence Manager role at Atlas Search would allow me to bring my expertise in dashboard development to your core operational initiatives. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my team-mentoring and data modeling skills. I regularly guided junior staff on data cleanup and SQL query optimization, ensuring that our underlying data models were robust enough to support long-term reporting goals. By standardizing our documentation and SOPs, I reduced new hire onboarding time by 50%, ensuring that the team remained agile as we scaled. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in operational analytics in working on large-scale data strategy at Atlas Search. I know that my technical proficiency and commitment to mentorship align with what it takes to succeed in the Business Intelligence Manager role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
